name: replication
context: Set up PostgreSQL streaming replication, logical replication, and high availability
info: This skill covers PostgreSQL replication for high availability and read scaling. Streaming replication sends WAL (Write-Ahead Log) records from a primary to one or more standby servers for an exact physical copy — used for failover and read replicas. Logical replication uses publications and subscriptions to replicate specific tables or data subsets — used for zero-downtime migrations, cross-version upgrades, and selective data distribution. High availability configurations use tools like Patroni, pg_auto_failover, or cloud-managed failover to automatically promote a standby when the primary fails. Monitoring replication lag and WAL position is critical for ensuring data consistency.
body:
# PostgreSQL Replication

Replication keeps copies of your data on multiple servers for high availability, read scaling, and disaster recovery. PostgreSQL supports both physical (streaming) and logical replication.

## Streaming Replication

Streaming replication sends WAL records from the primary to standby servers. The standby is an exact physical copy.

### Primary Configuration

```ini
# postgresql.conf on primary
wal_level = replica
max_wal_senders = 10
wal_keep_size = 1GB
hot_standby = on
```

```sql
-- Create replication user
CREATE ROLE replicator WITH REPLICATION LOGIN PASSWORD 'secure_password';
```

```ini
# pg_hba.conf — allow replication connections
host replication replicator replica_ip/32 scram-sha-256
```

### Standby Setup

```bash
# Take a base backup from the primary
pg_basebackup -h primary_host -U replicator -D /var/lib/postgresql/data -Fp -Xs -P -R

# The -R flag creates standby.signal and sets primary_conninfo in postgresql.auto.conf
# Start the standby — it will begin streaming WAL from the primary
pg_ctl start -D /var/lib/postgresql/data
```

### Verify Replication

```sql
-- On primary: check connected standbys
SELECT client_addr, state, sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn,
       sent_lsn - replay_lsn AS replication_lag
FROM pg_stat_replication;

-- On standby: check recovery status
SELECT pg_is_in_recovery(), pg_last_wal_receive_lsn(), pg_last_wal_replay_lsn();
```

## Logical Replication

Logical replication replicates specific tables using publications and subscriptions. Unlike streaming replication, it works across different PostgreSQL versions and allows selective data replication.

```ini
# postgresql.conf on publisher
wal_level = logical
max_replication_slots = 10
max_wal_senders = 10
```

```sql
-- On publisher: create a publication
CREATE PUBLICATION my_pub FOR TABLE users, orders;
-- or replicate all tables
CREATE PUBLICATION my_pub FOR ALL TABLES;

-- On subscriber: create a subscription
CREATE SUBSCRIPTION my_sub
  CONNECTION 'host=publisher_host dbname=mydb user=replicator password=secure_password'
  PUBLICATION my_pub;

-- Monitor subscription status
SELECT * FROM pg_stat_subscription;

-- Monitor replication slots
SELECT slot_name, active, restart_lsn, confirmed_flush_lsn
FROM pg_replication_slots;
```

## Failover

Manual failover for streaming replication:

```bash
# Promote standby to primary
pg_ctl promote -D /var/lib/postgresql/data

# Or via SQL
SELECT pg_promote();
```

Automated failover tools:

| Tool | Approach | Features |
|------|----------|----------|
| Patroni | etcd/Consul/ZooKeeper consensus | Industry standard, automatic failover, REST API |
| pg_auto_failover | Built-in monitor node | Simpler setup, PostgreSQL-native |
| Stolon | etcd/Consul consensus | Kubernetes-friendly |
| Cloud managed | Provider handles failover | AWS RDS, Cloud SQL, Azure — zero config |

### Patroni Example

```yaml
# patroni.yml
scope: postgres-cluster
name: node1

restapi:
  listen: 0.0.0.0:8008

etcd:
  hosts: etcd1:2379,etcd2:2379,etcd3:2379

bootstrap:
  dcs:
    ttl: 30
    loop_wait: 10
    retry_timeout: 10
    maximum_lag_on_failover: 1048576
  postgresql:
    parameters:
      wal_level: replica
      max_wal_senders: 10
      max_replication_slots: 10

postgresql:
  listen: 0.0.0.0:5432
  data_dir: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  authentication:
    replication:
      username: replicator
      password: secure_password
    superuser:
      username: postgres
      password: secure_password
```

## Monitoring

```sql
-- Replication lag in bytes
SELECT
  client_addr,
  pg_wal_lsn_diff(sent_lsn, replay_lsn) AS lag_bytes,
  state
FROM pg_stat_replication;

-- Replication lag in time (standby)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN pg_is_in_recovery()
    THEN EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - pg_last_xact_replay_timestamp()))
    ELSE 0
  END AS lag_seconds;

-- WAL generation rate
SELECT pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), '0/0') AS total_wal_bytes;
```

sections.title: Streaming replication
sections.body: Physical replication via WAL streaming. Set `wal_level = replica` on primary, create a replication user, use `pg_basebackup` with `-R` to initialize the standby. The standby continuously receives and replays WAL records. Hot standby allows read queries on the standby. See [[postgres/performance#config-tuning]] for WAL-related settings.

sections.title: Logical replication
sections.body: Table-level replication using publications and subscriptions. Set `wal_level = logical`. Publisher creates `PUBLICATION`, subscriber creates `SUBSCRIPTION`. Works across PostgreSQL versions. Supports selective table replication. Does not replicate DDL changes — schema changes must be applied manually on both sides. See [[postgres/queries]] for the SQL involved.

sections.title: Failover
sections.body: Manual failover with `pg_ctl promote` or `SELECT pg_promote()`. Automated failover with Patroni (industry standard, uses etcd/Consul for consensus), pg_auto_failover (simpler, PostgreSQL-native), or cloud-managed solutions. Patroni provides a REST API for cluster status and switchover operations. See [[postgres/backup]] for combining replication with backup strategies.

sections.title: Monitoring
sections.body: Monitor `pg_stat_replication` on the primary for connected standbys and lag. `pg_wal_lsn_diff()` computes lag in bytes. On the standby, `pg_last_xact_replay_timestamp()` shows when the last transaction was replayed — compare with `now()` for time-based lag. Alert when replication lag exceeds your RPO (Recovery Point Objective). See [[postgres/performance]] for general monitoring queries.

links.context: Combine replication with backups for full disaster recovery

scripts.description: Configure PostgreSQL streaming or logical replication between primary and replica
